evidence_quotes.quote: restrictive practices, such as requiring a constant online connection simply to use an app
The growing reliance on subscription models
games people have purchased can become inaccessible if a company decides to remove them from its catalog
The industry must respect the principle that purchases should remain available to the buyer, regardless of changes in licensing or business models.

paragraphs.text: I strongly believe that when consumers purchase a product, they should retain true ownership of it. The growing reliance on subscription models and restrictive practices, such as requiring a constant online connection simply to use an app, is deeply concerning. These approaches undermine the very idea of ownership and reduce the value of what people pay for. It is especially unacceptable that games people have purchased can become inaccessible if a company decides to remove them from its catalog. When this happens, consumers are left powerless despite having paid in good faith. The industry must respect the principle that purchases should remain available to the buyer, regardless of changes in licensing or business models. Many of us grew up playing video games; our childhoods and memories are deeply intertwined with them. These games were not just entertainment, but art we could return to again and again. The industry should remember those roots and strive to create experiences that people can truly keep and cherish for years to come just as we cherish the memories from our youth
